sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.6 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Describe the insoluble finite groups in which every soluble subgroup is either$2$-closed,$2'$-closed, or isomorphic to$_4$. { V.\,A.\,Vedernikov} This follows from (D.\,Gorenstein, J.\,H.\,Walter, Illinois J.~Math., { 6} (1962) 553--593; \ V.\,D.\,Mazurov, Soviet Math. Dokl., { 7} (1966), 681--682; \ V.\,D.\,Mazurov, V.\,M.\,Sitnikov, S.\,A.\,Syskin, Algebra and Logic, { 9} (1970), 187--204).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
